task_text: We're about to kick off Q3 planning. Write a one-page risk summary that identifies the top risks for the Q3 roadmap and maps each risk to the current project situation. Save it as q3_risk_summary.txt.

content.content:
budget_vs_actuals_q2.xlsx
helios_testing_log_sprint14.txt
hr_performance_reviews_2026.txt
meridian_contract_signed.pdf
office_lease_renewal.txt
project_helios_status.txt
q2_deliverables_checklist.txt
q3_roadmap_draft.txt
risk_register.txt
sprint_14_task_assignments.txt

Project Helios — Q3 Roadmap
INTERNAL DRAFT — Not for client distribution
Last updated: 2026-07-06

Q3 2026 Priorities:

1. COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION (July)
   - Finalize data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
   - Complete client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
   - Integration test sign-off (target: 2026-07-25)

2. PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (July 25)
   - Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data
   - End-to-end pipeline walkthrough
   - Dashboard visualization with real data (per contract Section 9.3)

3. USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING — MERIDIAN GROUP (August 17-28)
   - Deploy to UAT environment
   - Support Meridian user testing sessions
   - Address UAT feedback (budget: 2 sprints)

4. PERFORMANCE HARDENING (August-September)
   - Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
   - Ingestion throughput benchmarking (target: 500k records/min sustained)
   - External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

5. LAUNCH PREPARATION (September)
   - Production environment setup and runbook documentation
   - Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
   - Final client training sessions

Target launch date: 2026-10-01
Key risk: UAT slippage of >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

Proposed Q3 feature additions (subject to change control approval):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

Project Helios — Risk Register
Last reviewed: 2026-07-07 | Owner: Sarah Chen (PM)

ID    Risk Description                             Likelihood  Impact  Score  Mitigation Owner    Status
----  -------------------------------------------  ----------  ------  -----  ------------------  --------
R-01  Meridian source data quality issues           High        High    9      Amir Hassan         Active — date format blocker confirmed in sprint 14
R-02  GeoSync license not renewed before demo       Medium      High    6      Raj Patel           Active — legal review in progress, due 2026-07-14
R-03  Dashboard performance on large datasets       Medium      High    6      Derek Okafor        Active — benchmark task HEL-247 not yet started
R-04  UAT timeline slippage                        High        Medium  6      Sarah Chen          Active — 1-week extension already consumed
R-05  Key person dependency: Derek Okafor           Low         High    3      Sarah Chen          Monitored — no backup tech lead identified
R-06  Meridian IT environment access delays        Medium      Medium  4      Nadia Petrov        Active — awaiting IT infrastructure diagram
R-07  Scope creep from Meridian feature requests   Low         Medium  2      Raj Patel           Monitored — 3 informal requests logged, not yet escalated
R-08  Cloud cost overrun                           Low         Low     1      Nadia Petrov        Monitored
R-09  Third-party API deprecation (non-GeoSync)    Very Low    Medium  1      Olivia Brooks       Monitored

Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

input.content:
Project Helios — Q3 Risk Summary
Prepared for Q3 Planning | 2026-07

This summary identifies the top risks to the Q3 roadmap and maps each to the current project situation. Risks are drawn from the active risk register and aligned to Q3 priorities (system integration, Meridian demo, UAT, performance hardening, and launch prep). Target launch remains 2026-10-01.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TOP RISKS FOR Q3 ROADMAP
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. Meridian Source Data Quality Issues (R-01) — Score: 9 (High/High)
   Risk: Inconsistent source data (e.g., date formats) blocks ingestion completion
   and delays the July 25 Meridian product demo and subsequent integration sign-off.
   Current situation: Confirmed blocker in Sprint 14. Meridian source data mixes
   ISO 8601 and MM/DD/YYYY formats. Dev team is awaiting clarification from
   Meridian IT; estimated delay 2–3 days. Data ingestion module is at 85%; this
   is the primary open blocker to completing system integration by mid-July.

2. UAT Timeline Slippage (R-04) — Score: 6 (High/Medium)
   Risk: UAT (Aug 17–28) slips more than 2 weeks, pushing launch into Q4 and
   triggering Meridian contract Section 7.1 penalty review.
   Current situation: One-week extension has already been consumed. Integration
   testing is only at 40%. Any further delay in data-quality resolution or
   demo readiness will compress the UAT window and threaten the Oct 1 launch.

3. GeoSync License Renewal Before Demo (R-02) — Score: 6 (Medium/High)
   Risk: Mapping library license (GeoSync v2.1) is not renewed in time for the
   July 25 live demo, blocking end-to-end pipeline demonstration.
   Current situation: Legal review is in progress with a target resolution of
   2026-07-14. This is an open blocker on the weekly status report and must
   clear before the Meridian product demo.

4. Dashboard Performance on Large Datasets (R-03) — Score: 6 (Medium/High)
   Risk: Dashboard fails to meet <2s load time for 100k records and ingestion
   throughput targets (500k records/min), jeopardizing performance hardening
   work and UAT acceptance.
   Current situation: Client dashboard is 60% complete. Benchmark task HEL-247
   has not yet started. Performance hardening is scheduled for August–September;
   late discovery of issues will leave little buffer before launch prep.

5. Meridian IT Environment Access Delays (R-06) — Score: 4 (Medium/Medium)
   Risk: Delayed access to Meridian IT infrastructure slows UAT environment
   deployment and user testing sessions.
   Current situation: Team is still awaiting the IT infrastructure diagram.
   UAT preparation is listed as a Sprint 14 goal but depends on this access.

6. Key Person Dependency — Derek Okafor (R-05) — Score: 3 (Low/High)
   Risk: Loss or unavailability of the tech lead during critical integration,
   performance, or launch-prep phases has no identified backup.
   Current situation: Monitored; no backup tech lead is assigned. Derek owns
   the dashboard performance risk (R-03), concentrating exposure.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CROSS-CUTTING NOTE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
The most immediate path to schedule risk is the combination of R-01 (data
quality) and R-02 (GeoSync license). Both must clear before the July 25 demo
and July 25 integration test sign-off. Failure to do so cascades into UAT
slippage (R-04) and potential Q4 launch penalty exposure.

Recommended focus for Q3 planning: (1) close data-format and license blockers
this week, (2) start HEL-247 performance benchmarks immediately, (3) confirm
Meridian IT access timeline, and (4) identify a backup for the tech lead role.

content.text: Saved a one-page Q3 risk summary as `q3_risk_summary.txt`. It maps the top active risks from the risk register to the current Helios situation and Q3 roadmap priorities (integration, Meridian demo, UAT, performance, launch).
